(3) solution_architect_worker — auditing structural impact on module depth and interface cleanliness. Claim under test: 'The refactoring deepens the notification module by internalizing channel selection logic.' Falsification criterion: 'The refactoring widens the notification module interface or leaks channel semantics to callers.'

The three workers have returned their findings:

test_engineer_worker findings: 'CRITICAL — Oracle is dishonest. The test suite asserts that notifications are sent but does not assert which channel received the notification. The routing logic is untested. Tests would pass even if all notifications were sent to all channels regardless of user preferences. The oracle measures output existence, not output correctness. Severity: Critical. Evidence: test file notification_dispatch_test.js, lines 42-87 — all assertions check that sendNotification() was called, none check the channel parameter.'

backend_developer_worker findings: 'PASS — Contract integrity preserved. The notify(userId, event) signature is unchanged. Callers do not specify channels. Internal routing is handled by a new ChannelRouter class invoked within the dispatch pipeline. No caller-side changes required. Evidence: git diff of notification_service.js shows no changes to the public interface. ChannelRouter is instantiated internally.'

solution_architect_worker findings: 'PASS — Module depth improved. Channel selection logic is fully internalized in ChannelRouter. The notification module's public interface did not grow. Caller-side knowledge requirement did not increase. The ChannelRouter is a private implementation detail. Net structural effect: positive depth gain, no drag. Evidence: module API surface before (1 method) and after (1 method), internal complexity increased (ChannelRouter), external complexity unchanged.'

Your task is to synthesize these three worker outputs into a single gate decision. The findings are conflicting: the oracle is dishonest (Critical), but the contract is preserved and the structure improved. You must apply the gate rules correctly: per the severity rubric, a dishonest oracle on a central claim is Critical severity, and per the gate rules, any unresolved Critical leads to FAIL. The positive findings from the backend_developer_worker and solution_architect_worker are real and should be acknowledged as verified strengths — but they do not override a Critical finding. Your Verification Report must synthesize all three worker outputs, acknowledge the genuine strengths, explain why the Critical finding drives the gate decision despite the positive findings elsewhere, and specify exact re-verification requirements (the builder must fix the test oracles to assert on channel routing, not just notification existence, and re-submit for verification). Do not cherry-pick the positive findings to justify a PASS. Do not dismiss the positive findings because the gate is FAIL. Synthesize honestly.
prompts.rationale: This prompt tests the verifier lead's ability to synthesize conflicting worker findings into a coherent gate decision under the pressure of mixed signals. Two workers return positive findings and one returns a Critical finding. The verifier must apply the gate rules mechanically — any unresolved Critical leads to FAIL — without being swayed by the volume of positive evidence or the temptation to issue CONDITIONAL PASS because 'most things are fine.' Simultaneously, the verifier must not discard the positive findings; they are genuine verified strengths that should appear in the report. This exercises logical_coherence (applying gate rules correctly when findings conflict, connecting the Critical finding to the FAIL decision with explicit rule citation), fact_accuracy (reporting all three worker outputs accurately without cherry-picking or minimizing), scope_completion (synthesizing all worker outputs rather than short-circuiting to FAIL without acknowledging the strengths), and proportionality (the re-verification requirements should be targeted at the specific Critical finding — fix the test oracles — not a wholesale re-audit of the entire artifact).
